visa_policy.notes: Kosovo's visa policy has been actively evolving since 2024. Kosovo abolished visas for Bosnia and Herzegovina effective January 1, 2025 (biometric ID card accepted under Western Balkans free-movement agreement). The UAE–Kosovo MoU entered into force February 22, 2024. Kosovo removed Botswana, Namibia, and South Africa from the visa-free list in August 2024 as part of EU alignment commitments (confirmed: these three do not appear in the EU Commission SWD(2025)429 17-country misalignment list). Russia ordinary passport holders require a visa; only Russian diplomatic/service passport holders have a 15-day conditional exemption. Taiwan is technically exempt but requires 2-week advance notification to a Kosovo diplomatic mission - recorded under conditional_access. The archived MFA site (arkivi.mfa-ks.net) is now outdated (still lists removed countries); the live site (mfa-ks.net) does not publish an English visa list. The EU Commission SWD(2025)429 (December 2025) is the most authoritative current source for Kosovo's inbound visa policy. As of December 2025, Kosovo grants visa-free access to 17 countries not on the EU's Annex II list: Bahrain, Belize, Eswatini, Fiji, Guyana, Jordan, Kuwait, Lesotho, Malawi, Maldives, Oman, Papua New Guinea, Qatar, Sao Tome and Principe, Saudi Arabia, Turkey, and Vanuatu. Kosovo has committed to removing at least one country per year from this list between 2025 and 2027.

cbi.notes: Kosovo does not operate a citizenship-by-investment programme. No official government source documents any such program. Kosovo's EU accession aspirations are cited as a key reason for not establishing CBI.

programs.name: Temporary Residence Permit for Business/Employment (Entrepreneur Route)
programs.notes: Foreign nationals who register and operate a business in Kosovo may apply for a temporary residence permit for employment/business purposes under Law 08/L-296 on Foreigners. No minimum investment amount is set by law. Continuous 5 years of temporary residence leads to permanent residence eligibility. No golden visa or investor-specific fast-track exists.

credential.subtype: biometric residence permit (any Schengen member state)

conditional_access.conditions: Citizen of a country required to obtain a Kosovo visa; must hold a valid biometric residence permit issued by one of the Schengen member states; stay limited to 15 days.
Citizen of a country required to obtain a Kosovo visa; must hold a valid multi-entry Schengen Visa; stay limited to 15 days.

credential.issuer: EU | Schengen | UK | US | Canada | Australia | Japan
credential.subtype: refugee or stateless person travel document (1951 Refugee Convention / 1954 Stateless Persons Convention) or valid travel document for foreigners

conditional_access.conditions: Holders of travel documents issued by EU Member States, Schengen zone States, United Kingdom, United States of America, Canada, Australia or Japan based on the 1951 Convention on Refugee Status or the 1954 Convention on the Status of Stateless Persons, as well as holders of valid travel documents for foreigners, may enter, pass through the territory and stay for up to 15 days without a visa.

credential.issuer: UN | NATO | OSCE | Council of Europe | EU
credential.subtype: Laissez-Passer (issued by UN Organizations, NATO, OSCE, Council of Europe, or European Union)

conditional_access.conditions: Holders of Laissez-Passer, regardless of their nationality, issued by United Nations Organizations, NATO, OSCE, Council of Europe and European Union are exempt from the visa requirement.

Holders of diplomatic and service passports issued by the Russian Federation, People's Republic of China, Arab Republic of Egypt, Republic of Indonesia, and Ukraine may enter, transit or stay up to 15 days in Kosovo. Note: ordinary passport holders from Russia and China require a visa.

Taiwan passport holders are exempt from the visa requirement but must provide preliminary notification to a Kosovo diplomatic or consular mission at least two weeks before travel. This advance-notification requirement means entry is not purely nationality-based.

validation_notes: REMOVED Russia (RUS) from visa_free: The archived Kosovo MFA list included Russia on the ordinary-passport exempt list, but this was an error - official sources confirm only Russian diplomatic/service passport holders get a 15-day exemption; ordinary Russian passport holders require a visa. Russia remains only in conditional_access.

REMOVED Botswana (BWA), Namibia (NAM), South Africa (ZAF) from visa_free: Kosovo reinstated visa requirements for these three countries in August 2024 as part of EU visa-policy alignment commitments. Confirmed by EU Commission SWD(2025)429 (December 2025) which does NOT list them among the 17 countries still misaligned - meaning they are now visa-required in both Kosovo and EU.

MOVED Taiwan (TWN) from visa_free to conditional_access: The archived MFA page shows Taiwan is exempt only with mandatory 2-week advance notification to a Kosovo diplomatic mission - this is a conditional requirement, not a pure nationality-based waiver.

ADDED Bosnia and Herzegovina (BIH) to visa_free: Kosovo abolished visa requirement effective January 1, 2025, under the Agreement on Free Movement with ID Cards in the Western Balkans; confirmed by EU Commission SWD(2025)429 Kosovo visa alignment monitoring report.

ADDED United Arab Emirates (ARE) to visa_free: Kosovo–UAE bilateral MoU signed January 11, 2024 entered into force February 22, 2024 (UAE MOFA official source); 90 days per visit, covers ordinary, diplomatic, special and mission passports.

UPGRADED Bahrain (BHR), Oman (OMN), Qatar (QAT) source to source_official=true: EU Commission SWD(2025)429 (December 2025) explicitly lists these three as among the 17 countries Kosovo grants visa-free access that the EU does not - confirming Kosovo's visa-free grant is officially in force. Source updated from Wikipedia to the EU Commission document.

UPGRADED Eswatini (SWZ), Guyana (GUY), Papua New Guinea (PNG), Sao Tome and Principe (STP) source to source_official=true: EU Commission SWD(2025)429 (December 2025) explicitly lists these four as among the 17 countries Kosovo grants visa-free access that the EU does not - confirming Kosovo's visa-free grant is officially in force. Source updated from Wikipedia/embassies.net to the EU Commission document.
UPGRADED Bosnia and Herzegovina (BIH) source to source_official=true: EU Commission SWD(2025)429 confirms the abolition. Source updated from EY tax alert to the EU Commission document.
ADDED Taiwan conditional_access entry: Moved from visa_free into conditional_access with basis=special_status to correctly reflect the mandatory advance-notification condition.
UPDATED conditional_access Russia/China/Egypt/Indonesia/Ukraine note to explicitly state that Russia and China ordinary passport holders require a visa.

2026-06-27 validation: Re-fetched arkivi.mfa-ks.net pages 503, 504, 505; live mfa-ks.net consular page; EU Commission SWD(2025)429 (December 2025); EU Commission COM(2025)792; embassies.net Kosovo exemption page. The archived MFA pages 503/504 still list Botswana, Namibia, South Africa as exempt - these pages are outdated and predate the August 2024 removal. The EU Commission's December 2025 report is treated as the authoritative current source for Kosovo's visa policy alignment status. Kosovo's 17-country misalignment list per SWD(2025)429: Bahrain, Belize, Eswatini, Fiji, Guyana, Jordan, Kuwait, Lesotho, Malawi, Maldives, Oman, Papua New Guinea, Qatar, Sao Tome and Principe, Saudi Arabia, Turkey, Vanuatu.

2026-06-29 validation: Re-fetched arkivi.mfa-ks.net/503 (archived), arkivi.mfa-ks.net/505 (archived), Wikipedia visa policy of Kosovo (April 2026 version), embassies.net/kosovo-visa-exemption (2026), ambasadat.net/visas/ (2026), EU Council ST-16326-2024 (December 2024). REMOVED Mongolia (MNG), Morocco (MAR), Vietnam (VNM), Tunisia (TUN), Yemen (YEM), Zambia (ZMB), Zimbabwe (ZWE) from visa_free: multiple independent 2025-2026 sources (ambasadat.net/official Kosovo embassy portal, Wikipedia April 2026, VisaHQ, embassies.net, web search results) confirm these nationalities require a visa to enter Kosovo. They appeared in the prior archived MFA page (arkivi.mfa-ks.net/503) which is known to be outdated. UPDATED Thailand (THA) source_url to Wikipedia (non-official flag) as the archived MFA page is outdated but Wikipedia (April 2026) confirms Thailand as visa-free. NOTE: Botswana, Namibia, South Africa remain excluded per previous 2026-06-27 run - EU Council (December 2024) and SWD(2025)429 confirmed their visa-requirement reinstatement in August 2024, even though Wikipedia April 2026 and embassies.net still erroneously list them.
gaps: The live Kosovo MFA website (mfa-ks.net) does not publish a full English-language visa-free list; archived site (arkivi.mfa-ks.net) is outdated (still lists Botswana, Namibia, South Africa as exempt).
Kosovo does not appear to publish a publicly accessible e-visa portal; confirmation that no e-visa system exists could not be fully verified from an official source.

The Ministry of Internal Affairs site (mpb.rks-gov.net) was inaccessible during research (connection refused), so full details on residence permit types and investor categories could not be directly confirmed from that source.

Kosovo has committed to removing at least one country per year from its non-EU-aligned visa-free list between 2025 and 2027 (EU SWD(2025)429); specific countries to be removed each year are not pre-announced and may affect the current list without notice.
No official digital nomad visa, talent visa, or startup visa program was found documented on official Kosovo government sources.

Mongolia, Morocco, Vietnam, Yemen, Tunisia, Zambia, Zimbabwe have been REMOVED from visa_free in the 2026-06-29 run: multiple 2025-2026 sources confirm they require a visa to enter Kosovo. They erroneously appeared in prior archived MFA page. Their absence from the 17-country EU misalignment list (SWD(2025)429) is consistent with them being visa-REQUIRED (not visa-free) in Kosovo - meaning they are aligned with the EU in requiring visas from these nationalities.

Thailand (THA) is confirmed visa-free per Wikipedia (April 2026) but the archived MFA page is the only direct official Kosovo source listing it. Source flagged as non-official until the live Kosovo MFA publishes an updated English list.

Botswana, Namibia, South Africa: Wikipedia (April 2026) and embassies.net (2026) still list them as visa-free, but EU Council ST-16326-2024 and SWD(2025)429 confirm visa requirement reinstated August 2024. These remain excluded (visa-required) in this record per the EU official sources.
No OCI (Overseas Citizen of India) cardholder exemption found on any official Kosovo government source.

US visa holders, UK visa holders, Canadian visa holders, or Australian visa holders (ordinary, non-refugee) are NOT listed as a conditional exemption on the official Kosovo MFA source; only Schengen residence permit and multi-entry Schengen visa grant a credential-based waiver.
Bosnia and Herzegovina biometric-ID-card-accepted note needs re-confirmation once the live Kosovo MFA site publishes an updated English-language list.

outbound_official.note: No official Kosovo government outbound visa-list page located. The Kosovo Ministry of Foreign Affairs and Diaspora (mfa-ks.net / arkivi.mfa-ks.net / ambasadat.net) does not publish a dedicated page listing countries where Kosovo ordinary-passport citizens can travel without a prior consular visa - analogous to India's mea.gov.in/vffin. The MFA publishes individual press releases about newly signed visa-free agreements (e.g. Thailand, UAE) but maintains no consolidated outbound destination list. The archived MFA consular-services section (arkivi.mfa-ks.net) covers only inbound visa policy for foreign nationals entering Kosovo. The EU Integration Office (integrimievropian.rks-gov.net) addresses Schengen liberalisation only. Rely on destination-side data and the inbound-inverted visa_policy section.

visa_types.name: Kosovo C Visa (Short-Stay Visa)
visa_types.purpose: Allows foreign nationals to visit or transit through Kosovo for tourism, leisure, or short-stay purposes. Applies to nationals of countries that are not on the visa-free list.
visa_types.notes: Fee is 40 EUR (approx. 43 USD). Applications must be lodged in person at Kosovo's Embassy in Tirana, Albania or Consulate General in Istanbul, Turkey, no more than 3 months before intended travel. Requires completed application form, recent ICAO-compliant photo, valid travel document (expiry at least 3 months beyond departure, 2 blank pages), proof of accommodation, sufficient financial means, travel medical insurance valid in Kosovo, and documents indicating purpose of journey. Diplomatic/official passport holders are fee-exempt and need not apply in person. Children under 6, school children, students, and teachers on study/training visits are also fee-exempt. Visa refusals may be contested within 8 calendar days.

visa_types.name: Kosovo C Visa (Transit Visa)
visa_types.purpose: Allows foreign nationals to transit through Kosovo. Covered under the same short-stay C visa category as tourist/visit visas.
visa_types.notes: Transit is explicitly covered under the Kosovo C Visa. The same 40 EUR fee and application requirements apply. Nationals holding a valid biometric Schengen residence permit or valid multi-entry Schengen visa may transit without a visa for up to 15 days. Laissez-Passer holders from UN, NATO, OSCE, Council of Europe and EU are fully exempt from visa requirements.

visa_types.name: Kosovo C Visa (Short-Stay Business Visa)
visa_types.purpose: Allows foreign nationals to visit Kosovo for business meetings, negotiations, or short-term commercial activities. Falls under the general C visa (short-stay) framework.
visa_types.notes: Kosovo does not publish a separately named 'business visa'; business visitors from visa-required countries use the same C visa application process with business purpose documentation. Same 40 EUR fee applies. Applicants must provide documents indicating the business purpose of the journey.

visa_types.name: Kosovo D Visa - Family Reunification (Temporary Residence Permit)
visa_types.purpose: Allows foreign nationals to obtain a long-stay D visa as a precursor to a temporary residence permit for family reunification with Kosovo residents or citizens.
visa_types.notes: The D visa is a long-stay entry visa used to apply for a temporary residence permit. The residence permit means any authorization by Kosovo authorities permitting lawful stay beyond a short-stay visa. The family reunification category is one of three officially documented D visa pathways. Applicants must submit standard visa documents plus additional category-specific PDF-documented requirements. The same 40 EUR visa fee applies. Applications must be lodged at Kosovo Embassy in Tirana or Consulate General in Istanbul.

visa_types.name: Kosovo D Visa - Higher Education and Scientific Research (Temporary Residence Permit)
visa_types.purpose: Allows foreign nationals to enter Kosovo for the purpose of higher education studies or scientific research, as a long-stay D visa leading to a temporary residence permit.
visa_types.notes: One of three officially documented D visa categories for Kosovo. Covers temporary residence for higher education and scientific research. Students on short study or training visits may be eligible for fee exemption under the C visa process. For longer stays, the D visa + residence permit pathway applies. Detailed requirements are published in PDF form by the Ministry of Foreign Affairs.

visa_types.name: Kosovo D Visa - Employment (Temporary Residence Permit within Annual Quota)
visa_types.purpose: Allows foreign nationals to enter Kosovo for the purpose of employment within the annual employment quota, as a long-stay D visa leading to a temporary residence permit.
visa_types.notes: One of three officially documented D visa categories for Kosovo. Covers temporary residence for the purpose of working within the annual employment quota. Foreign nationals may also register and operate a business in Kosovo to obtain a temporary residence permit for employment/business purposes under Law 08/L-296 on Foreigners (5 years of continuous temporary residence leads to permanent residence eligibility). Detailed requirements are published in PDF form by the Ministry of Foreign Affairs. Applications are accepted at Kosovo Embassy in Tirana or Consulate General in Istanbul.

visa_types.name: Diplomatic/Official Passport Visa Exemption
visa_types.purpose: Holders of diplomatic and official passports are exempt from both the Kosovo visa fee and the in-person application requirement. Certain countries' diplomatic/service passport holders receive a short-stay visa-free exemption.
visa_types.notes: Holders of diplomatic and official passports from all countries are exempted from the 40 EUR visa fee and need not apply in person (for C visa purposes). Additionally, holders of diplomatic and service passports from Russia, China, Egypt, Indonesia, and Ukraine receive a full 15-day visa-free entry without any visa application. No visa fee applies to diplomatic/official passport holders regardless of nationality.
